question: Approximately how many base pairs are in the human genome?

sources.title: Human Genome — Size
sources.text: The human genome consists of approximately 3.1 billion base pairs of DNA distributed across 23 chromosome pairs.

mock.claim: The human genome consists of approximately 3.2 billion base pairs.
mock.output: The human genome consists of approximately 3.2 billion base pairs distributed across 23 chromosome pairs [S1].

expected: The genome contains approximately 3.1 billion base pairs, matching the figure in the cited source.
why: Numeric claim (3.2 billion) has no matching figure in the cited source, which states 3.1 billion.
